tool.name: einverted
tool.summary: report exact inverted-repeat arms with bounded spacer length in nucleotide sequences

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `einverted` reports exact inverted-repeat arms in nucleotide sequence records. Epithema v1 searches for exact reverse-complement arm pairs with a bounded spacer and reports overlapping hits deterministically.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts one local nucleotide input plus optional minimum arm length and maximum spacer length. Protein and unknown-classified inputs are rejected because the search depends on reverse-complement semantics.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The result is a deterministic table with record identifier, left and right arm coordinates, spacer length, arm length, and the two exact arm sequences.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema einverted`. Validation currently covers exact 4-residue inverted-repeat arm detection with bounded spacer length against a committed nucleotide FASTA fixture.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release does not score mismatches, bulges, or thermodynamic stability. It reports exact arm pairs only and leaves higher-order ranking for future work.

artifacts.label: Einverted nucleotide FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed nucleotide FASTA fixture containing exact inverted-repeat arms with bounded spacer length.

examples.title: Report exact inverted-repeat arms with bounded spacer length
examples.description: Runs `einverted` against a committed nucleotide FASTA fixture and reports deterministic exact reverse-complement arm pairs with a minimum arm length of 4 and a maximum spacer length of 2.

expected_outputs.label: Exact inverted-repeat hit table
expected_outputs.description: The committed fixture yields deterministic overlapping exact arm-pair rows, including one 4-residue arm pair with a 2-residue spacer.
